title: AST SpaceMobile Gains Verizon Ally in Direct-to-Device Competition
text: Verizon joined AT&T in signing a definitive agreement to use AST SpaceMobile's planned space-based cellular network. AST SpaceMobile signed a definitive commercial agreement with Verizon to provide direct-to-cellular service when needed for Verizon customers starting in 2026. Both US operators signed agreements with AST SpaceMobile to use their 850MHz low-band spectrum to connect customers across the continental US. The space-to-cell connection is designed to work transparently, meaning customers won't need to worry about which carrier's 850MHz spectrum is being used. The deal enables Verizon to provide direct-to-device connectivity to its customers from some point in 2026, building on a strategic partnership announced in May 2024 that included plans for a $100 million investment in AST SpaceMobile. This partnership positions ASTS as a key player in direct-to-device satellite services alongside traditional telecom operators.
